title: Whisked Chinese Freekeh Stir-fry
description: A light and sour date-night Chinese dish featuring fresh freekeh, perfectly whisked to complement the vibrant flavors.

ingredients.name: 1 cup uncooked freekeh
ingredients.notes: Prepared by cooking rice in a mixture of wheat and barley, lightly toasted.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Lightly sweetened to enhance the dish's flavor profile.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: To add a tangy and refreshing touch.

ingredients.name: 2 scallions, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: To garnish the finished dish with greenery.

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Used for stir-frying and enhancing the overall texture.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a pop of color and additional freshness.

ingredients.name: 1 small red bell pepper, diced
ingredients.notes: Adds vibrant color and slight sweetness to the dish.

instructions: Preheat your wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add oil for cooking.
In a separate bowl, whisk together soy sauce, lemon juice, minced garlic, and salt (if needed). Set aside.
Add sliced freekeh to the hot pan, tossing gently with the reserved whisked mixture until evenly coated. Remove from heat momentarily.
Stir in diced red bell pepper for about 2 minutes until they start to soften slightly but still retain their crunchiness.
Return to the wok or skillet and quickly mix in scallions, cilantro (if using), and any residual whisked sauce over medium-high heat. Ensure everything is well combined.
Serve hot as a light side dish or main course. Garnish with more fresh herbs if desired.
Let it rest for about 1 minute before serving to allow flavors to meld together.
